tasks.title: Provider collection/auth seam
tasks.definition_of_done: opi-ai exposes a provider collection/auth contract that owns provider and model lookup, static API-key and env-auth descriptors, optional refresh capability, OpenAI-compatible compatibility metadata, stream dispatch, an explicit complete-dispatch decision compatible with the current streaming Provider trait, redacted missing/invalid auth diagnostics, and a registry regression asserting all built-in providers still resolve; no OAuth login, subscription auth, broad catalog expansion, or image generation is implemented.

inference_notes.reason: Expanded Workstream 10.1 into concrete provider collection, auth, metadata, dispatch, diagnostics, and non-goal assertions.
Phase 10 starts after Phase 9 documentation guards and baseline realignment have passed.

No provider OAuth login, subscription auth, broad provider catalog expansion, image generation, custom TUI extension protocol, npm/package marketplace work, browser/web UI work, pi TypeScript API compatibility, pi session file compatibility, shared opi-types crate, or whole-loop rewrite.
Replaced the vague existing-provider "work" regression with a named registry assertion, and surfaced the stream/complete dispatch decision required by Workstream 10.1.

Workstream 10.1 decisions D1 (evolve ProviderRegistry versus wrap it in a new collection type) and D2 (resolve auth per request, run, or turn snapshot) are implementation-time design choices; any choice is acceptable only if SC1, SC2, and SC4 remain satisfied.

Task 10.1 adds opi_ai::provider_collection under crates/opi-ai/src, which trips the existing Phase 6 guard first_class_provider_set_is_unchanged in productized_packages_docs.rs. The guard classifies every non-provider opi-ai/src module against an infra allowlist; provider_collection is infrastructure (the collection/auth seam), not a new first-class provider, so the infra allowlist must be extended. No new provider is added (Phase 6 non-goal preserved).

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: A library caller builds the opi-ai provider collection, looks up a configured model, resolves static or env-described auth without exposing secret values, and receives a provider stream through the collection dispatch path.
Provider correctness tests can exercise collection-level model lookup, compatibility metadata, auth diagnostics, and stream dispatch without constructing the CLI product harness.
The ProviderRegistry regression named registry_resolves_all_builtin_providers resolves every built-in provider/model family after the provider collection/auth seam is introduced.

last_attempt.note: Single TDD red-green cycle; one commit.

evidence.opi_verification: library; cargo test -p opi-ai (all suites, 0 failed) + provider_collection_dispatches_with_redacted_auth + collection_supports_provider_correctness_fixtures + registry_resolves_all_builtin_providers green; clippy -p opi-ai --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; rustdoc -p opi-ai -D warnings clean; fmt --check --all clean; workspace clippy + rustdoc -D warnings clean; bash scripts/opi-impl-smoke.sh PASSED
evidence.opi_acceptance: phase10-provider-collection-auth-seam, phase10-provider-fixture-target, phase10-existing-provider-regression; ProviderCollection + ProviderRegistry exercised via dispatch_stream/dispatch_complete/resolve/capabilities/auth_status/compat plus the all-builtin-providers registry regression

2026-06-25: appended productized_packages_docs.rs to task_owned_paths (C.1a) to extend the infra allowlist for provider_collection.

tasks.title: Coding-agent provider factory routing
tasks.definition_of_done: opi-coding-agent provider construction routes CLI config, env, package/custom provider inputs, model listing, and run-mode startup through the opi-ai provider collection/auth seam; the provider_policy_is_centralized regression scans crates/opi-coding-agent/src and asserts ProviderRegistry::new, resolve_model_spec, and equivalent provider/model/auth policy calls appear only in the provider_factory allowlist; existing --list-models, non-interactive, JSON, RPC, and interactive startup behavior remains covered by regression tests.

inference_notes.reason: Expanded Workstream 10.1 coding-agent responsibilities into factory routing, product-owned inputs, behavior preservation, and regression tests.
The product factory must consume the opi-ai provider collection/auth seam from task 10.1.

No provider OAuth login, subscription auth, broad provider catalog expansion, image generation, custom TUI extension protocol, npm/package marketplace work, browser/web UI work, pi TypeScript API compatibility, pi session file compatibility, shared opi-types crate, or whole-loop rewrite.
Replaced an unobservable anti-scattering claim with the named provider_policy_is_centralized grep-style regression.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: The opi binary startup paths construct providers from CLI config/env/package extension inputs through the opi-ai provider collection, preserve existing list-models and run-mode behavior, and keep provider/model/auth policy calls inside the provider_factory allowlist.
Existing provider startup paths for non-interactive JSON/RPC-style product flows continue to work through the collection-backed factory.

last_attempt.note: Single TDD cycle: centralized construction into provider_factory.rs producing ProviderCollection; rewired harness (model_registry -> ProviderCollection) + main (run-mode + list-models) through the factory; grep regression + routes-through-collection tests; one commit.

tasks.title: Generic AgentHarness core seam
tasks.definition_of_done: opi-agent exposes a documented generic AgentHarness seam covering idle/turn/compaction/branch-summary phase guards, turn snapshots, save points, runtime config mutation for future snapshots, pending session write ordering, busy rejections, queue safe points, cancellation cleanup, and abort semantics with contract tests.

inference_notes.reason: Expanded Workstream 10.2 generic harness semantics into executable contract assertions.
Generic harness work starts after Phase 9 establishes the AgentHarness gap and Phase 10 scope.

No provider OAuth login, subscription auth, broad provider catalog expansion, image generation, custom TUI extension protocol, npm/package marketplace work, browser/web UI work, pi TypeScript API compatibility, pi session file compatibility, shared opi-types crate, or whole-loop rewrite.
Selected the Phase 10 real-type delivery path and removed the accepted-design escape hatch so substrate_only=false matches executable harness tests and production call-site claims.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: A non-CLI library caller can exercise the generic harness phase, snapshot, save-point, pending-write, and runtime-config semantics without depending on opi-coding-agent product policy.
Contract tests prove phase guards, busy rejections, queued operations, cancellation cleanup, and session write ordering through the generic opi-agent harness surface.

last_attempt.note: Single TDD red-green cycle; one commit. RED = unresolved import opi_agent::harness; GREEN = harness module + 2 contract tests.

evidence.opi_acceptance: phase10-generic-harness-seam, phase10-harness-contracts; generic_harness_phase_snapshot_savepoint_contract (non-CLI caller via opi_ai MockProvider: phase/snapshot/savepoint/pending-write + runtime-config snapshot discipline proven both halves, agent-before-extension durable order via SessionReader::read_all) + phase_guards_busy_rejections_and_abort_cleanup (begin_*/end_*/enqueue/flush phase guards, runtime-config setters never gated, abort cancels agent token + best-effort flush, flush-failure preserves queue count, abort-with-failed-flush returns AbortLeftPending(n) and resets to Idle with writes queued)

session_notes: 2026-06-26: started task 10.3 (auto-pick). Design via read-only discovery workflow (4 Explore agents + opus synthesis) + substrate verification. New opi-agent::harness module: AgentHarness wraps Agent + HarnessSession backend, owns Phase machine (Idle/Turn/Compaction/BranchSummary), PendingWriteQueue (agent-emitted before extension/session writes), HarnessRuntimeConfig snapshot discipline (future snapshots only), SavePoint/HarnessSnapshot, abort() flushes all accepted pending writes. Reuses Agent/AgentLoopConfig/SessionWriter/SessionEntry; no agent.rs changes. begin_turn/end_turn are state-machine guards + snapshot/flush in 10.3 (full loop wiring = 10.4). BranchSummary phase guarded but entry representation deferred to 10.5 (user-confirmed Proceed as planned). start_commit=c069853.

2026-06-26: task 10.3 PASSED @ a644adc. New opi_agent::harness module: AgentHarness wraps Agent + Box<dyn HarnessSession>, owns Phase machine (Idle/Turn/Compaction/BranchSummary), PendingWriteQueue (drain_ordered: AgentMessage-kind before ExtensionState-kind, stable by order), HarnessRuntimeConfig/Builder with snapshot discipline (begin_turn freezes turn_snapshot; snapshot() returns frozen during Turn, live builder otherwise; setters affect future turns only), SavePoint/HarnessSnapshot, abort() cancels agent + best-effort flushes all accepted writes (never discards; on flush failure returns AbortLeftPending(n), still resets to Idle, keeps queue). HarnessSession trait + JsonlHarnessSession impl over SessionWriter. Reuses Agent (delegated cancel_token/control_handle/messages_snapshot/abort), AgentLoopConfig, SessionWriter/SessionReader, SessionEntry/MessageEntry/CompactionEntry/ExtensionStateEntry, CompactionResult. No agent.rs changes (begin_turn is a guard + snapshot freeze; loop wiring is 10.4). BranchSummary phase guarded but no durable entry (representation deferred to 10.5). Types kept module-path public (opi_agent::harness::*) following session-module precedent -> avoided Phase 8 crate-root API-surface-classification churn. Design via read-only discovery Workflow (4 Explore + opus synth). D.2 evaluator PASS (no blocking; 3 info nits). All cross-cutting gates green. No push.

inference_notes.reason: Expanded CodingHarness wrapper success criteria into production startup and behavior-preservation assertions.

Existing CLI, RPC, JSON, and interactive-facing product flows retain their current observable behavior unless a contract test exposes an existing bug.

session_notes: 2026-06-26: started task 10.4 (auto-pick). Design via read-only discovery Workflow (4 Explore + opus-intent synthesis) + independent design-doc read. Synthesis recommended production AgentHarness adoption + begin_turn/end_turn bracket, but that is infeasible: AgentHarness::new takes Agent BY VALUE (harness.rs:330) and Agent is not Clone (Box<dyn AgentHooks> hooks, Arc<Mutex<Vec<Box<dyn Fn>>>> subscribers) -> product cannot keep driving Agent::prompt while handing Agent to AgentHarness; that path dead-ends into either adding a loop-driving method to the generic seam (Phase 8 classification risk + scope creep) or replacing SessionCoordinator with the harness session model (Workstream 10.3 / task 10.5, deferred). Rejected synthesis doc-edits: docs/pi-alignment-matrix.md (owned by 10.7, not in task_owned_paths) and the Phase 10 design doc (would change ledger hash -> spec drift; opi-implement forbids mutating normative specs). tasks.title: Session repo/facade boundaries
tasks.definition_of_done: opi-agent defines session entry, repo/storage trait, and harness session-facade boundaries for messages, compaction, leaf links, model/thinking changes, labels, branch summaries, custom entries, ordered append/load/list/fork behavior, unknown future entry handling, deterministic branch leaf reconstruction, pending write ordering, and durable-state ownership; the implementation explicitly decides additive-v1 versus session-v2 entry strategy, branch-summary representation, extension custom-message provider-context handling, and unfinished-operation recovery without replaying unsafe tools; existing v1 sessions remain readable and Phase 13 has a stable seam to extend.

inference_notes.reason: Expanded Workstream 10.3 into concrete session entry, repo, facade, v1 compatibility, branch reconstruction, pending-write, and durable-state assertions.
Session facade semantics use the generic harness save-point and pending-write model from task 10.3.

No provider OAuth login, subscription auth, broad provider catalog expansion, image generation, custom TUI extension protocol, npm/package marketplace work, browser/web UI work, pi TypeScript API compatibility, pi session file compatibility, shared opi-types crate, or whole-loop rewrite.
Surfaced Workstream 10.3 required decisions for session versioning, branch summaries, extension custom messages, and unfinished-operation recovery.
Removed normative docs from this code task; Phase 10 spec and alignment documentation updates remain owned by task 10.7.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: A generic opi-agent session repo/facade can append and read durable entries in order, preserve v1 session readability, reconstruct the active branch leaf deterministically, and leave product directory policy in opi-coding-agent.
Pending session writes accepted during busy phases flush in documented order at save points and operation settlement without silently dropping accepted writes on abort.

tasks.title: Runtime hook boundary hardening
tasks.definition_of_done: Runtime hook boundaries are documented and tested so core loop hooks remain narrow in opi-agent, coding-agent product extensions/resources/packages stay in opi-coding-agent, process adapter hosting remains out of opi-agent, typed hook result composition is tested where it affects runtime behavior, and provider request/response hooks plus custom TUI UI/message renderer hooks are deferred with explicit prerequisites.

inference_notes.reason: Expanded Workstream 10.4 into current/future hook boundary documentation, typed composition tests, and process-adapter ownership assertions.
Hook boundary documentation must reflect both generic harness and CodingHarness wrapper ownership.

No provider OAuth login, subscription auth, broad provider catalog expansion, image generation, custom TUI extension protocol, npm/package marketplace work, browser/web UI work, pi TypeScript API compatibility, pi session file compatibility, shared opi-types crate, or whole-loop rewrite.
Changed from docs to refactor because this task owns Rust hook-boundary tests and may adjust typed hook composition code.

Editing docs/opi-spec.md (owned) changes its SHA256; the phase4 ledger drift-detector test phase4_ledger_spec_hash_matches_current_spec compares the current spec hash against the frozen docs/snapshots/phase4/opi-impl-state.json, so that snapshot must be resynced to keep the workspace test green. Per spec-edit-breaks-phase4-ledger: only the phase4 snapshot needs the update.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: The documented and tested extension/hook surfaces distinguish current core runtime hooks, coding-agent product extensions, process adapter responsibilities, and future provider/UI/session lifecycle hooks without claiming pi TypeScript API compatibility, and a positive source-structure assertion proves process adapter protocol parsing stays out of opi-agent.
Hook result composition that changes runtime behavior is typed and covered by tests for ordering, short-circuiting, and product adapter boundaries.

last_attempt.note: Single TDD cycle (RED: phase10_runtime_hook_boundaries failed on missing section -> GREEN: dedicated spec section EN+ZH + guards). C.1a phase4 snapshot resync. No production runtime change.

Final guards and phase gates run after every Phase 10 runtime/documentation slice is passing.

No provider OAuth login, subscription auth, broad provider catalog expansion, image generation, custom TUI extension protocol, npm/package marketplace work, browser/web UI work, pi TypeScript API compatibility, pi session file compatibility, shared opi-types crate, or whole-loop rewrite.

task_summary.title: Docs guards and final Phase 10 gates

audit_notes: Residual (non-blocking, original design intent, not deferred-by-updated-design): SC2 runtime dispatch path (assemble_harness_collection -> ProviderCollection::from_registry) does not auth-gate the active provider; only --list-models attaches AuthDescriptors. Documented intent (provider_factory.rs:11-25); becomes load-bearing if Phase 11/12 needs runtime auth enforcement.

Residual (non-blocking): SC3 begin_turn/begin_compaction are state-machine guards only; the agent loop is not yet routed through AgentHarness (deferred to task 10.4+/Phase 11 per harness.rs:30-36). Branch-summary durable entry representation deferred (task 10.5 D2, harness.rs:706-709).

Residual (non-blocking): SC5 Phase 13 richer entry variants (branch_summary durable, extension-message context injection, unfinished-operation replay) deferred per D2/D3/D4 (harness.rs:705-719); additive-v1 non_exhaustive SessionEntry + forward-compatible reader is the boundary contract. Consistent with SC5 wording.

Residual (non-blocking): SC8 OAuth grep hits are all doc-comment deferrals or upstream bearer-token passthrough (provider_collection.rs:25-29 'Future OAuth not implemented'; vertex.rs:6 / config.rs:161 Vertex OAuth2 bearer token user-supplied via VERTEX_ACCESS_TOKEN; streaming_proxy.rs:337 GitHub token-type passthrough; bedrock/credentials.rs:143 'no SSO browser/device flow'). No opi OAuth login / subscription auth / image generation / browser-web / npm-marketplace / pi-TS-compat / pi-session-compat / opi-types-crate / whole-loop-rewrite implemented.

Dual hash convention applied at 10.6 + 10.7 (spec edits): phase4 snapshot CRLF-normalized (phase4_ledger pins it); live ledger raw sha256sum. phase6 snapshot carries spec_files_sha256 but NO test consumes it (memory 'phase6 empty' was stale; corrected post-task).
